name: Forced-Perspective Moving Day
description: Pick up ordinary objects and release them against surfaces so their physical scale becomes their apparent screen size, turning tiny signs into bridges and huge crates into slot-sized keys.
natural_language: Resize the available objects through perspective and move the shipment through the impossible doorway. Shrink the crate into its keyed floor slot, enlarge the sign into a load-bearing bridge, then navigate the rigid camera body across the void and through the unlocked door.

metadata.variant_count_kind: mirror, camera yaw, focal length, palette, projected object positions, and depth-scale ratios
metadata.notes: A real pinhole projection and inverse camera-depth placement derive every released world scale; avatar support, door, object, wall, and void collisions replay independently.
